title: Handwriting Characteristics Comparison
description: Identify the key handwriting characteristics — Line Quality, Slant, Spacing, Letter Formation, Pen Lifts, and Baseline — used to compare a questioned writing sample with a known exemplar. Click a characteristic to highlight it on both samples, toggle the questioned sample between authentic and forged, and use the magnification slider to look closer.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Questioned Document Examination
Handwriting Analysis
Forgery Detection
Document Forensics
bloomLevel: Remember (L1) — identify the characteristics used to compare handwriting samples.

educational.topic: Characteristics used to compare questioned and known handwriting samples
learningObjectives: Identify the six characteristics document examiners compare: Line Quality, Slant, Spacing, Letter Formation, Pen Lifts, and Baseline.
Recognize how a forgery differs from an authentic sample in each characteristic.
Read a comparison summary reporting matching versus differing characteristics.
educational.prerequisites: None beyond familiarity with the idea that everyone's handwriting is distinctive

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Two side-by-side samples — a known exemplar and a questioned sample — render the same simulated cursive writing. A row of characteristic chips lets the student click any of the six characteristics to highlight it on both samples (blue on the exemplar, green for a match or red for a difference on the questioned sample). A toggle switches the questioned sample between authentic and forged (the forgery adds tremor, slant change, baseline drift, and hesitation pen lifts). A magnification slider zooms both samples.
pedagogical.assessment: The student selects each characteristic and reads its label, description, and a MATCH/DIFFERS badge, plus a running comparison summary (matching vs. differing characteristics) that updates between the authentic and forged states.
pedagogical.modelType: Procedurally generated cursive stroke path parameterized by slant, tremor, baseline wave, loop spacing, and pen-lift positions, with deterministic seeds so the authentic sample closely matches the exemplar and the forged sample diverges in five of six characteristics.
controls: Click a characteristic chip to highlight it on both samples
Show Forged / Show Authentic Sample toggle button
